    refine ae_eq_of_forall_mem measurableSet_univ ?_ |>.symm.le
    intro x _
    simp [h3, h4]
    <;>
    (try
      simp_all [probSpace, Measurable, MeasurableSet])
    <;>
    (try
      exact Set.univ_mem)
    <;>
    (try
      exact Set.mem_univ _)
    <;>
    (try
      simp_all [probSpace, Measurable, MeasurableSet])
    <;>
    (try
      exact Set.univ_mem)
    <;>
    (try
      exact Set.mem_univ _)
  have h5 : IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x ↦ hist alg x n) (arrayMeasure ν) :=
    h1.of_right_le h2
  exact h5

have h1 : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    exact indepFun_fst_snd ν
  have h2 : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := h1
  have h3 : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => hist alg x n) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h4 : Function.Indep (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) := by
      apply Function.indep_iff_indepFun.mp h2
    have h5 : Function.Indep (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) := h4
    have h6 : Function.Indep (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => hist alg x n) := by
      refine h5.of_right_imp _ |>.trans _
      · -- Show that the function (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) can be used to recover (fun x : probSpace α R => hist alg x n)
        have h7 : (fun x : probSpace α R => hist alg x n) ∘ (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) = (fun x : probSpace α R => hist alg x n) := by
          funext
          <;> simp [Function.comp_apply]
        have h8 : (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) = (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) := rfl
        have h9 : Function.Indep (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) := h5
        -- Use the independence to conclude the required independence
        have h10 : Function.Indep (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) := h5
        -- This part is not directly meaningful but is used to fit the form of the theorem
        simpa [h7] using h10
      · -- Show that the function (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) implies (fun x : probSpace α R => hist alg x n)
        -- This is trivially true because (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) is a part of the composition
        have h7 : Function.Indep (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) := h5
        have h8 : Function.Indep (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) := h5
        -- Use the independence to conclude the required independence
        have h9 : Function.Indep (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => x.2) := h5
        -- This part is not directly meaningful but is used to fit the form of the theorem
        simpa [Function.comp_apply] using h9
    -- Use the independence to conclude the required independence
    have h11 : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x : probSpace α R => hist alg x n) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      apply h6.of_respectively_ae
      <;> simp_all [IndepFun]
      <;> try norm_num
      <;> try simp_all [Function.comp_apply]
    exact h11
  -- Use the independence to conclude the required independence
  exact h3

have h₁ : IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R)) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h₂ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ ω.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      -- Use the fact that the first and second components are independent in the product measure
      have h₃ : IndepFun (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ ω.1) (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ ω.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
        -- The product measure is independent by definition
        exact indepFun_prod measurable_id measurable_id
      -- Use the independent function transformation to get the desired result
      have h₄ : (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) = (fun ω : (ℕ → I) ↦ ω (n + 1)) ∘ (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ ω.1) := by
        ext ω
        rfl
      rw [h₄]
      -- Apply the independent function transformation
      have h₅ : BorelFunction (fun ω : (ℕ → I) ↦ ω (n + 1)) := by
        exact measurableEvaluation (n + 1)
      exact h₃.comp (φ := (fun ω : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ ω.1)) (by simp [h₅]) measurable_id
    exact h₂
  -- Use the fact that the reward function is independent of the next component
  have h₂ : IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R)) (arrayMeasure ν) := h₁
  -- Transform the independent function to the desired form
  have h₃ : (fun x ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R)) = (fun x ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R)) := rfl
  have h₄ : IndepFun (fun ω ↦ ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun x ↦ @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    have h₅ : (fun x ↦ @Bandits.ArrayModel.hist α R mα mR inst✝⁶ inst✝⁵ inst✝⁴ alg x n) = (fun x ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R) n (initAlgFunction alg (x.1 0))) := by
      rfl
    rw [h₅]
    -- Use the independent function transformation to get the desired result
    have h₆ : BorelFunction (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R) n (initAlgFunction alg (x.1 0))) := by
      have h₇ : Measurable (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R) n (initAlgFunction alg (x.1 0))) := by
        have h₈ : Measurable (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ x.1 0) := by
          -- The first component is measurable
          have h₉ : Measurable (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ x.1) := by
            exact measurable_fst
          exact measurably h₉ (measurableEvaluation 0)
        have h₉ : Measurable (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R) n) := by
          -- The second component is measurable
          have h₁₀ : Measurable (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ x.2) := by
            exact measurable_snd
          exact measurably h₁₀ (measurableEvaluation n)
        -- The composition is measurable
        have h₁₀ : Measurable (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R) n (initAlgFunction alg (x.1 0))) := by
          apply Measurable.comp
          · exact h₉
          · exact measurableEvaluation (initAlgFunction alg (x.1 0))
          · exact measurable_id
        exact h₁₀
      exact h₇.borel
    -- Apply the independent function transformation
    have h₇ : (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R) n (initAlgFunction alg (x.1 0))) = (fun x ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R) n (initAlgFunction alg (x.1 0))) := rfl
    exact h₂.comp (φ := (fun x : (ℕ → I) × (ℕ → α → R) ↦ (x.2 : ℕ → α → R) n (initAlgFunction alg (x.1 0)))) (by simp [h₆]) (by simp [h₆])
  exact h₄

have h₁ : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    -- Use the fact that the sigma-algebra of the second component is independent of the sigma-algebra of the first component
    exact indepFun_fst_snd ν

  have h₂ : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
    -- Show that the sigma-algebra of (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n) is contained in the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)
    have h₃ : Measurable (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) := by
      have h₄ : Measurable (fun ω : probSpace α R => Π (i : ℕ), α × R) := by
        -- The product space is measurable
        exact measurable_prod_countable (fun _ => by infer_instance)
      -- Compose with the measurable function (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)
      exact measurable_of_forall_meaasurable_of_smul (by
        intro i
        have h₅ : Measurable (fun ω : probSpace α R => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg ω n i) := by
          apply measurable_hist
        exact h₅
      )
    -- Use the fact that the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) is independent of the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1))
    have h₄ : (induced (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) nullMeasurableSet : σAlgebra (probSpace α R)) ≤ (induced (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) nullMeasurableSet : σAlgebra (probSpace α R)) := by
      -- Show that the sigma-algebra of (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n) is contained in the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)
      have h₅ : (induced (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) nullMeasurableSet : σAlgebra (probSpace α R)) = (MeasurableElements (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n))) := by
        -- The sigma-algebra of (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n) is the elements of the sigma-algebra of (probSpace α R) that are measurable with respect to (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)
        rfl
      have h₆ : (induced (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) nullMeasurableSet : σAlgebra (probSpace α R)) = (MeasurableElements (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)) := by
        -- The sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) is the elements of the sigma-algebra of (probSpace α R) that are measurable with respect to (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)
        rfl
      have h₇ : MeasurableElements (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) ≤ MeasurableElements (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) := by
        -- Use the fact that (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n) is measurable and the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) is the same as the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)
        have h₈ : Measurable (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) := by
          exact h₃
        have h₉ : Measurable (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) := by
          -- The function (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) is measurable
          exact measurable_snd
        have h₁₀ : (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) = (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) ∘ (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) := by
          ext ω
          rfl
        -- Use the fact that the composition of measurable functions is measurable
        have h₁₁ : Measurable ((fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) ∘ (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n))) := by
          exact h₉.comp h₃
        -- Use the fact that the sigma-algebra of (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n) is contained in the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)
        have h₁₂ : MeasurableElements (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) ≤ MeasurableElements (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) := by
          -- Use the fact that (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n) is measurable and the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) is the same as the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)
          refine' MeasurableElementLe _
          intro s
          simp only [MeasurableElements.mem_setOf_eq]
          have h₁₃ : Measurable (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) := by
            exact h₃
          have h₁₄ : Measurable (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) := by
            exact measurable_snd
          simp_all [h₁₀]
          <;>
          aesop
        exact h₁₂
      -- Use the fact that the sigma-algebra of (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n) is contained in the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)
      have h₁₃ : (induced (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) nullMeasurableSet : σAlgebra (probSpace α R)) ≤ (induced (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) nullMeasurableSet : σAlgebra (probSpace α R)) := by
        -- Use the fact that the sigma-algebra of (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n) is contained in the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)
        simp_all [h₅, h₆]
        <;>
        aesop
      exact h₁₃
    -- Use the fact that the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2) is independent of the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1))
    have h₅ : IndepFun (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) (arrayMeasure ν) := by
      refine' IndepFun.of_mono_right (fun ω => ω.2) h₁ _
      -- Use the fact that the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n)) is contained in the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.2)
      exact h₄
    exact h₅
  -- Use the fact that the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => ω.1 (n + 1)) is independent of the sigma-algebra of (fun ω : probSpace α R => (fun x => @hist α R _ _ _ _ _ alg x n))
  exact h₂
